name: Gate3587Bug
description:
Issue #3587 demo. Demonstrates the parked-work-never-recovers bug: if the Deacon session dies after `bd gate check` closes a gate but before `gt gate wake <id>` runs, parked polecats never receive wake notification and wait indefinitely.

Topology:
  - Pol (Polecat): receives Park from Parker, transitions Absent -> Parked. Receives Wake on WAKE_IN to transition Parked -> Working. SLING/HOOK_IN bound to NeverFires stubs (no slings or compaction in this demo).
  - Iss (Issue): bound for Polecat's ISSUE_OBSERVE; stays Open throughout (no SLING).
  - Pkr (Parker): autonomous; fires Park once at t=0.
  - Gt (Gate): starts Open; transitions to Closed when Deacon sends Close.
  - Dec (Deacon): runs the two-step protocol -- close gate, then send wake. Has an explicit crash transition WakeReady -> Crashed that models 'Deacon session dies after bd gate check closes the gate but before gt gate wake runs'.

The critical bug interleaving: Deacon transitions Idle -> ClosingGate (sends Close) -> WakeReady (observes Gate=Closed) -> Crashed. Gate is Closed, no Wake in flight, Polecat stays Parked. The PolecatEventuallyWorking liveness property fails because TLC finds the crash branch.

No fix in this spec; the companion gate-3587-fix.kinner.json adds PrimeRecovery for the proposed self-heal.

Real sources: cmd/bd/gate.go:818 closeGate(), internal/cmd/gate.go:84 gt gate wake, internal/cmd/prime.go (where the proposed checkParkedWork() would land).

properties.description: Liveness: under fairness, the parked polecat eventually reaches Working. Fails in this bug spec because Deacon's WakeReady -> Crashed branch leaves Polecat without a wake.
